channel.title: Deuteronomy on Christian Internet Resources
channel.description: Recent content in Deuteronomy on Christian Internet Resources

item.title: Deuteronomy 1
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 1

Conversational Yahveh Covenant

The Command to Leave Horeb

 1:1Moses spoke to Israel in the open country east of the Jordan, in the Arabah across from Suph between Paran and Tophel, Laban, Hazeroth, and Dizahab. 2 It takes 11 days to travel from Horeb/Sinai past Mount Seir to Kadesh-barnea. 3 The first day, eleventh month, fortieth year, Moses told Israel everything Yahveh had told him to give them. 4 That was after he’d defeated Sihon, king of the Amorites, in Heshbon and Og, king of Bashan, in Ashtaroth and Edrei. 5 East of the Jordan in Moab, Moses undertook to explain the Law.

item.title: Deuteronomy 2
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 2

Israel’s Wanderings Remembered

1 “We turned around and set out for the desert toward the Red Sea like Yahveh instructed me, and we circled Mount Seir for a long time. 2 He told me, 3 ’You’ve circled this mountain long enough; turn north. 4 Tell the people, “Go through the territory of your relatives, Esau’s descendants, who live in Seir. 5 Don’t stir them up; I won’t give you a foot of their land. I’ve given Mount Seir to Esau. 6 Buy food and water from them so you can eat and drink. 7 Yahveh has blessed you in everything you’ve done. He has known your wanderings through this big desert. He’s been with you 40 years. You haven’t lacked anything.”’

item.title: Deuteronomy 3
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 3

Defeat of King Og

1 “Then we turned and went up the highway to Bashan. Og king of Bashan and his people came out to battle at Edrei. 2 But Yahveh told me, ‘Don’t be afraid of him. I’ll deliver him, his people, and his land to you. Do what you did to Sihon.’ 3 He delivered Og and his people to us. We didn’t leave any survivors.

item.title: Deuteronomy 4
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 4

Moses Commands Israel to Obey

1 “Listen, Israel, to the statutes and case laws I’m teaching you so you can take over the land. 2 Don’t add to or take away from what I’m telling you. 3 You’ve seen what Yahveh did to you at Baal-peor: he destroyed everyone that followed Baal-peor. 4 But every one of you is alive today that held onto Yahveh your God. 5 I’ve taught you statutes and case laws like Yahveh commanded me so you’d do them in the land you’re entering. 6 Keep them; that’s your wisdom and understanding in front of the nations that will hear those statues and say, ‘This great nation has wisdom and understanding.’ 7 What great nation has a god as near it as Yahveh is when we call on him? 8 What great nation has statutes and case laws as good as this Law I’m setting in front of you today?

item.title: Deuteronomy 5
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 5

The Ten Commandments

1 Then Moses called Israel together,

“Listen to these statutes and ordinances so you can learn them and observe them carefully. 2 Yahveh our God made a covenant with us at Horeb/Sinai. 3 He didn’t make that covenant with our ancestors, but with us. 4 Out of the fire he spoke to you directly at the mountain 5 while I stood between him and you. I stood there to tell you what he said, because you were afraid of the fire and didn’t go up the mountain. He said,

item.title: Deuteronomy 6
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 6

Call to Commitment

1 “Yahveh your God told me to teach you those statutes 2 so you and your descendants would respect him and keep them your whole life and live a long time. 3 Do them, Israel, so things will go well for you, and you’ll multiply in a land flowing with milk and honey.

4 “Listen, Israel, Yahveh is our God, Yahveh only. 5 Love him with all your heart, soul, and strength. 6 Keep in mind the things I’m commanding you. 7 Teach them diligently to your children. Talk about them when you’re sitting at home, walking down the road, lying down, or getting up. 8 Tie them like signs on your hand and make them like frontlets on your forehead. 9 Write them on your gates and doorposts.

item.title: Deuteronomy 7
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 7

Driving Out the Nations

1 “Yahveh will take you to the land and clear out the Hittites, Girgashites, Amorites, Canaanites, Perizzites, Hivites, Jebusites—seven nations bigger and stronger than you. 2 He’ll deliver them to you, defeat them, and destroy them. Don’t make treaties with them or show them mercy 3 or intermarry with them. 4 They’ll turn your children away from following him to serving other gods. His anger will flare and soon destroy you. 5 Tear down their altars, smash their sacred pillars, cut down their Asherah poles, burn their carved images.

item.title: Deuteronomy 8
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 8

Call Not to Forget the LORD

1 “Do every commandment I’m giving you today, so you’ll live and multiply, and take over the land. 2 Remember the way Yahveh led you in the desert those 40 years to humble you. He was testing you to see if you were committed to obeying him. 3 He let you get hungry, then fed you with manna, which you and your ancestors had never seen. He made you understand that a person doesn’t live by bread alone, but by everything Yahveh says. 4 All that time your clothes didn’t wear out; your feet didn’t swell. 5 He was disciplining you like a father disciplines his children. 6 So follow his commandments and respect him. 7 He’s taking you to a good land with streams and fountains, springs flowing in the hills and valleys. 8 It’s a land of wheat and barley; grapevines, fig trees, and pomegranates; olive oil and honey; 9 a land where there’s plenty of food, whose stones contain iron, whose hills have copper you can mine. 10 When you eat till you’re full, bless Yahveh for the good land he’s given you.

item.title: Deuteronomy 9
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 9

Victory by God’s Power

1 “Israel, you’re crossing the Jordan now to take over nations bigger and stronger than you, to take over big cities fortified to the sky, 2 to take over people big and tall, the Anakim you’ve heard people say about, ‘Who can stand up to the descendants of Anak?’

3 “Understand that it’s Yahveh your God who’s crossing over ahead of you like a consuming fire. He’ll conquer and destroy them so you can drive them out quickly.

item.title: Deuteronomy 10
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 10

The Second Stone Tablets

1 “At that time, Yahveh said, ‘Carve out your own two stone tablets like the first ones, come up to me on the mountain, and make a wooden Ark. 2 I’ll write on the tablets what was on the ones you shattered. Put them in the Ark.’ 3 I made an Ark out of acacia wood and hewed out two stone tablets like the first ones and carried them up the mountain. 4 He wrote the Ten Commandments on them that he’d given you on the mountain out of the fire when you assembled. He gave them to me. 5 I came down and put the tablets in the Ark, and there they are.”

item.title: Deuteronomy 11
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 11

Love and Obey the LORD

1 “Love Yahveh and always keep his ordinances. 2 I’m not speaking to your sons today. They haven’t seen his discipline, his greatness, his strong hand and outstretched arm; 3 his signs and works against Pharaoh, his country, 4 his army, its horses and chariots. He made the Red Sea overwhelm them when they chased you. 5 Your children didn’t see what he did to you in the desert before you got here. 6 They didn’t see what he did to Dathan and Abiram, Eliab’s sons, when the ground swallowed them, their families, tents, and everything living with them. 7 But you have seen his great work.

item.title: Deuteronomy 12
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 12

One Central Place of Worship

1 “You’re to observe these statutes and judgments as long as you live. 2 Destroy the places where these nations worship their gods—on hills and mountains and under every green tree. 3 Tear down their altars, smash their sacred pillars, burn their Asherah poles, cut down the carved images of their gods, erase their name from those places.

item.title: Deuteronomy 13
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 13

Warning Against Idolatry

1 “If a prophet or dreamer arises and gives you a sign or wonder 2 that comes true, and uses it to say, ‘Let’s serve other gods,’ 3 ignore him. Yahveh’s testing you to see if you love him with all your heart and soul. 4 Follow him, respect him, keep his commandments, listen to him, serve him, hold onto him. 5 Execute that prophet or dreamer. He has advocated rebellion against Yahveh who brought you out of Egypt and redeemed you from slavery. He’s seducing you to leave Yahveh. That’s how you’re to purge evil from among you.

item.title: Deuteronomy 14
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 14

Ceremonially Clean and Unclean Animals

                  1 “You’re the people of Yahveh your God. Don’t cut yourselves or shave your eyebrows for the dead. 2 You’re set apart to Yahveh. Out of all the nations, he’s chosen you to be his nation.

3 “Don’t eat anything detestable. 4 You can eat these animals: cattle, sheep, goats, 5 deer, gazelles, roebucks, wild goats, ibexes, antelopes, mountain sheep. 6 You can eat any animal that has divided hoofs and chews their cud. 7 Don’t eat camels, rabbits, and rock-badgers. They chew the cud, but don’t divide the hoof. They’re unclean. 8 Don’t eat pigs. They divide the hoof, but don’t chew cud. They’re ceremonially unclean. Don’t touch their carcasses.

item.title: Deuteronomy 15
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 15

The Year for Canceling Debts

1 “At the end of every seven years, cancel debts. 2 Creditors are to let go of what they’ve loaned to their fellow Hebrews. Don’t exact it from them; Yahveh has canceled the debt. 3 You can exact it from a foreigner, but not from fellow citizens. 4 There won’t be any poor among you because Yahveh will bless you like he promised 5 if you obey this commandment. 6 Lend to other nations, but don’t borrow from them. You’ll rule other nations; they won’t rule you.

item.title: Deuteronomy 16
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 16

Passover Festival

1 “In the month Abib, celebrate Passover to Yahveh your God. That’s when he brought you out of Egypt at night. 2 Sacrifice the Passover from the flock and the herd where he designates. 3 For a week, only eat bread without yeast—the bread of suffering (you came out in a hurry)—so you’ll remember all your life when you left Egypt. 4 For a week no yeast will be anywhere in any of your houses. None of the meat you sacrifice the first evening is to remain till morning. 5 You can’t sacrifice the Passover anywhere except where Yahveh designates for his name. 6 Sacrifice it at sundown, which is when you left Egypt. 7 Cook it and eat it where he designates. In the morning go back home. 8 For six days, eat bread with no yeast. On the seventh, hold a solemn assembly to Yahveh. Don’t do any work that day.

item.title: Deuteronomy 17
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 17

1 “Don’t sacrifice an ox or sheep that has a blemish. Yahveh detests that.

2-4 “In any of your towns, if you hear about a man or woman that breaks the covenant by serving other gods or worshiping the sun, moon, or stars, verify that detestable thing thoroughly. 5 Then take the person to your city gates and stone that man or woman to death. 6 You need the testimony of two or three witnesses to execute anyone. Don’t execute anybody on the word of one witness. 7 The accuser needs to throw the first stone. Then the people will join in. That’s how you’re to purge evil from among you.

item.title: Deuteronomy 18
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 18

Offerings for Priests and Levites

1 “The Levitical priests won’t inherit territory. They’ll eat Yahveh’s offerings by fire and his portion. 2 He is their inheritance like he promised.

3 “This is what the people owe the priests. When they offer a sacrifice—ox or sheep—they’ll give the priest the shoulder, two cheeks, and stomach. 4They’ll give him the first part of the harvest of their grain, new wine, and olive oil, as well as the first shearing of their sheep. 5 Out of all the tribes, Yahveh has permanently chosen Levi and his descendants to stand and serve in his name.

item.title: Deuteronomy 19
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 19

Cities of Refuge

1 “When Yahveh cuts off the nations whose land he’s giving you and you settle in their towns and houses, 2 set aside three towns, 3 build roads to them, and section the land into three parts so a person that’s killed someone can flee to them.

4 “This provision is for someone that kills another accidentally to flee to and live. 5 It’s for cases like when a couple friends go to the woods to cut wood. When one of them swings the axe to cut down a tree and the iron head comes off and kills the friend, the slayer can flee to one of these towns. 6 That way the avenger of blood can’t pursue in the heat of anger and take the slayer’s life. The slayer doesn’t deserve death because it didn’t happen because of hate. 7 So set aside three towns. [ 8 If Yahveh enlarges your territory and gives you the land he promised your ancestors, 9 and you always love him and follow his ways, then add three more towns.] 10 That way you won’t become guilty of shedding innocent blood.

item.title: Deuteronomy 20
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 20

Going to War

1 “When you go to battle and see horses, chariots, and people more numerous than you, don’t be afraid. Yahveh your God, who brought you out of Egypt, is with you. 2 When you’re getting ready for battle, the priests will come to remind you, 3 ‘Don’t be fainthearted, Israel. Don’t panic or tremble. 4 Yahveh is with you to fight for you and save you.’ 5 The officers will also say,

item.title: Deuteronomy 21
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 21

Ceremonial Cleansing for Unsolved Murder

1 “If a slain body turns up in open country and nobody knows who killed it, 2 your elders and judges will figure how far it is to the towns nearby. 3 The elders in the nearest town will take a heifer that hasn’t been worked, 4 and go down to a valley with running water—a valley that hasn’t been plowed or sown—and break her neck. 5 Then the priests, the Levites, will come—because Yahveh has chosen them to serve him—to bless in his name, to settle assaults. 6 The elders in that city will wash their hands over the heifer 7 and say, ‘We haven’t shed this blood and don’t know who did. 8 Forgive your people that you’ve redeemed, Yahveh. Don’t hold the guilt for innocent blood against us.’ He’ll forgive the murder. 9 That’s how you’ll remove the guilt for murder when you do what God considers right.

item.title: Deuteronomy 22
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 22

1 “Don’t watch your fellow citizen’s ox or sheep stray off and not take it to its owner. 2 If the owner doesn’t live close by or you don’t know who the owner is, take it home till the owner comes looking for it and then return it. 3 Do the same thing with someone’s donkey, piece of clothing, or anything a person lost. You can’t confiscate it. 4 Don’t watch your fellow citizen’s donkey or ox fall down on the road and not help it get it up.

item.title: Deuteronomy 23
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 23

Exclusion from the Assembly

1 “Nobody that’s been castrated or has his penis cut off, can go into Yahveh’s assembly. 2 No illegitimate person or his descendant can go into Yahveh’s assembly for ten generations. 3 No Ammonite, Moabite, or their descendant can go into Yahveh’s assembly for ten generations. 4 They didn’t meet you with food and water when you came out of Egypt. They hired Balaam Ben-Beor from Mesopotamia to curse you, 5 but Yahveh wouldn’t listen to him. He turned the curse into a blessing because he loves you. 6 Never seek peace or prosperity with them.

item.title: Deuteronomy 24
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 24

1 “When a man gets married and his wife becomes displeasing to him because he finds something wrong with her, he’s to give her a certificate of divorce and send her away. 2 If she marries another man 3 and he divorces her or dies, 4 her first husband can’t marry her again because she’s been defiled. That would be detestable to Yahveh. Don’t bring sin on the land he’s giving you.

item.title: Deuteronomy 25
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 25

1 “When people go to court and the judges decide their case, 2 if the offender deserves to be scourged, the judge will make the offender lie down and be scourged in his presence the number of stripes appropriate to the offence. 3 He can scourge him 40 times at most. More than that would be despising him.

item.title: Deuteronomy 26
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 26

Firstfruits and Tithes

1 “Once you’re living in the land, 2 put some of its first produce in a basket and go where Yahveh chooses to put his name. 3 Go to the priest and say,

‘I declare to Yahveh my God that I’ve entered the land he swore he’d give us.’ 4 The priest will set the basket down at the altar. 5 You’ll answer, ‘My ancestor was an Aramean nomad. He went down to Egypt and lived few in number, but he became a big strong nation there. 6 The Egyptians abused us with hard labor, 7 and we cried out to our ancestors’ God. He heard us and saw our misery, toil, and oppression. 8 He brought us out of Egypt with a strong hand and outstretched arm, with terror, signs, and wonders. 9 He brought us here and gave us this land, flowing with milk and honey. 10 I’ve brought the first produce of the ground you’ve given me.’ Set it down by Yahveh and worship him there. 11 You, the Levite, the foreigner among you will celebrate over the good he’s given you and your family.

item.title: Deuteronomy 27
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 27

The Altar on Mount Ebal

1 Then Moses and the elders in Israel charged the people,

“Keep the commandments I’m giving you. 2 When you cross the Jordan, set up large stones coated with lime. 3 Write this Law on them clearly so you can go to the promised land that’s flowing with milk and honey. 4 Set up these stones on Mount Ebal. 5 Build an altar there out of uncut stones—don’t use any iron tools on them. 6 Offer a burnt offering to Yahveh on it, 7 and sacrifice peace offerings. Eat there and celebrate in his presence.” [ 8 ]

item.title: Deuteronomy 28
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 28

Blessings for Obedience

1 “If you diligently obey Yahveh your God, he’ll set you high above every nation on earth. 2 These blessings will come on you:

3 “Blessed you’ll be in the city and the country.

4 “Blessed will be your offspring,

the produce of your ground,

the offspring of your animals,

item.title: Deuteronomy 29
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 29

Renewal of the Covenant

1 Yahveh told Moses to make this covenant with Israel in Moab besides the covenant he made with them at Horeb/Sinai. 2 Moses called Israel together and said,

            “You’ve seen what Yahveh did in Egypt to Pharaoh, his officials, and his land. 3 You saw the great trials, signs, and wonders. 4 Yet he still hasn’t given you a heart to know, eyes to see, or ears to hear. 5 I’ve been leading you for 40 years in the desert. Your clothes and sandals haven’t worn out. 6 You didn’t eat bread or drink wine or alcohol, but he provided for you so you would know he’s Yahveh your God. 7 When you got here, Sihon and Og came out to meet us, but we defeated them. 8 We gave their land to Reuben, Gad, and the Manasseh half-tribe. 9 So keep this covenant so you can prosper in everything you do.

item.title: Deuteronomy 30
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 30

Prosperity for Turning to the LORD

1-2 “When these blessings and curses have come down on you, and you and your descendants remember them where Yahveh banishes you, and you change and obey him with all your heart and soul— 3 then he’ll show you mercy and bring you back from captivity in the nations where he’s scattered you. 4 Even if you’re far away, he’ll return you 5 to your ancestors’ land to possess it again. He’ll prosper you and multiply you more than your forebears. 6 He’ll circumcise your and your descendants’ hearts so you’ll love him heart and soul, and have life. 7 Yahveh will inflict these curses on people that hate you and persecute you. 8 You’ll obey him again in everything I’m telling you now. 9 Then he’ll prosper you in what you do, in the offspring from your body and your livestock, and in the produce from your ground. He’ll delight in you again like he did in your ancestors. [ 10 ]

item.title: Deuteronomy 31
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 31

Joshua to Succeed Moses

1 Moses went and told all Israel,

2 “I’m 120 years old today. I can’t do like I used to, and Yahveh has told me I won’t cross the Jordan. 3 He’llY cross ahead of you and destroy those nations, and you’ll take over their land. Joshua will cross ahead of you like Yahveh said. 4 Yahveh will do to them what he did to Sihon, Og, and their lands. 5 He’ll deliver them up to you, and you’ll do to them like I’ve commanded you. 6 Be strong and courageous; don’t tremble with fear at them. Yahveh’s going with you. He won’t fail you or abandon you.”

item.title: Deuteronomy 32
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 32

            1 “Listen, sky; let me speak;  

      earth, hear what I say.

2 Let my teaching fall like rain;

let my speech distill like dew,

      like drops on fresh grass,

      like showers on plants;

                                                      3because I’m proclaiming Yahveh.

Credit greatness to our God, the Rock!                      Deut 31:30-32:1-3

item.title: Deuteronomy 33
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 33

Moses Blesses the Tribes

1 Moses the man of God gave this blessing to Israel before he died:         

2 “Yahveh came from Sinai

and dawned from Seir;

      he shined out from Mount Paran.

He came with ten thousand holy ones

with flashing lightning at his right hand.

item.title: Deuteronomy 34
item.description:
Deuteronomy Chapter 34

The Death of Moses

1 Moses went up from the plains of Moab to Mount Nebo, to the top of Pisgah across from Jericho. Yahveh showed him the land, Gilead to Dan; 2 Naphtali, Ephraim and Manasseh; the land of Judah to the Mediterranean Sea; 3 the Negev and the Valley of Jericho, the City of Palms as far as Zoar. 4 He told him, “This is the land I swore to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, ‘I’ll give it to your descendants.’ I’ve let you see it, but not enter it.” 5 So Moses died in Moab. 6 Yahveh buried him in an unknown place in a valley in Moab across from Beth-peor. 7 Although he was 120 when he died, his sight was still clear, and his energy was good. 8 Israel mourned him for 30 days in the plains of Moab.
